items.title: Improve recommender: exclude already-taken courses + user avoid list + exact target credits
items.content:
Context
- Current skill works end-to-end (setup/login/recommend) but still needs stronger personalization.

Requested improvements
1) Auto-exclude already-taken courses (교필 포함)
   - Parse taken course codes from total_grade.jsp (cumulative grades) and filter them out from recommendation candidates (both major + GE).
   - This avoids recommending 교필/교양 already completed (e.g., 사고와 표현/디자인씽킹/영어커뮤 등).

2) Support user avoid list / hard exclusions
   - Allow passing a list of course names/codes to exclude (e.g., 웹프로그래밍).
   - Provide a simple CLI flag in recommend_this_term.py and wire through openclaw/recommend_this_term.sh.

3) Improve exact target-credit matching
   - Handle 2-credit courses so final selection hits target (e.g., 18) exactly (avoid 17/19 cases).

Notes
- Keep read-only behavior (no registration writes).
- Keep outputs copy-paste friendly (md/ascii timetable).

items.title: feat: Add Deneb compatibility layer (v1.48) with search modes & version probing
items.content:
## Summary

- **Problem:** Deneb (parent project) needs to discover Vega's capabilities, control search behavior (FTS-only vs. vector vs. hybrid), and negotiate protocol compatibility.
- **Why it matters:** Enables seamless integration between Deneb and Vega with version negotiation, feature detection, and flexible search strategies.
- **What changed:** Added `memory-version` command, extended `memory-status` with capabilities/models/counts, implemented `--mode` flag for `memory-search` (search/vsearch/query), and added version/protocol constants.
- **What did NOT change:** Core search algorithms, database schema (SCHEMA_VERSION still 6), or existing command signatures—all changes are additive and backward compatible.

## Change Type

- [x] Feature
- [x] API / contracts

## Scope

- [x] Memory / storage
- [x] API / contracts

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related to Deneb(OpenClaw) integration requirements

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

1. **New `memory-version` command** — Lightweight endpoint returning version, protocol version, and capabilities (semantic search, reranking, search modes).
2. **Extended `memory-status` response** — Now includes `version`, `protocolVersion`, `capabilities` (with `searchModes`, `schemaVersion`, `inferenceBackend`, `rerankMode`), `models` (embedder/reranker/expander availability), and `counts` (projects/memoryFiles).
3. **`memory-search --mode` flag** — Three modes:
   - `search`: FTS5 only (fast, low recall)
   - `vsearch`: Vector search only (medium speed/recall)
   - `query`: Hybrid FTS + vector + reranking (slow, high recall, default)
4. **Config additions** — `VERSION = '1.48'` and `PROTOCOL_VERSION = 1` for version negotiation.

- New permissions/capabilities? **No** — All endpoints are read-only or existing operations.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No**
- New/changed network calls? **No**
- Command/tool execution surface changed? **No** — Only added new read-only commands and optional flags.

items.title: OpenClaw/NemoClaw feature integration (9 features + security hardening)
items.content:
## Summary

Integrates 9 features from OpenClaw/NemoClaw into NexiBot across 4 phases, with a security audit pass that found and fixed 6 issues.

### Phase 1: Foundation
- **F1: Tool Execution Sandboxing** — Wires existing Docker sandbox into 6-gate pipeline (new Gate 6), adds `SandboxFallback` enum, per-tool `sandbox_policy` config
- **F7: Security Audit CLI** — `nexibot security audit` with `--deep`, `--fix`, `--format json` flags
- **F8: Session JSONL Truncation** — Atomic write (temp+rename), configurable `max_transcript_entries: 200`

### Phase 2: Architecture
- **F2: Plugin SDK Architecture** — Bridge SDK v2 (`PluginSDK` class) + Rust native traits (`NexiBotPlugin`, `ToolPlugin`, `ProviderPlugin`), backward-compatible v1 support
- **F3: Declarative Network Policy** — YAML-based outbound allowlists with wildcard host matching, hot-reload, managed policy merge

### Phase 3: Intelligence
- **F4: Pre-Compaction Memory Flush** — Silent LLM turn before compaction to extract durable facts, 30s timeout, fail-open
- **F5: External Skill Format Discovery** — Auto-discover OpenClaw/Codex/Claude skill bundles via `SkillFormatAdapter` trait

### Phase 4: Integration
- **F6: Inference Routing Through Bridge** — JSONL inference logger, Google Gemini + DeepSeek bridge plugins (SDK v2), `use_bridge` config
- **F9: Multi-Agent Workspace Isolation** — Per-agent `sessions/skills/memory/scratch/` directories with scratch limits

### Security Hardening (Deep Analysis Pass 1)
- Path traversal prevention in `AgentWorkspace::new()` (whitelist `[a-zA-Z0-9_-]`)
- API key pattern sanitization in inference log error messages
- Private IP detection in network policy engine (DNS rebinding defense)
- Plugin registry `capabilities()` double-call bug fix
- ContextManager config hot-reload subscriber added
- Inference log directory path traversal validation

## Test plan
- [ ] `cargo check -p nexibot-cli` passes (verified)
- [ ] Existing unit tests pass for modified modules
- [ ] New tests pass

items.title: fix(sandbox): include workspace dir in container name to prevent cross-instance collisions
items.content:
## Problem

When multiple OpenClaw instances run on the same host with different `HOME` dirs and Docker sandbox enabled, they all produce the **same container name** (e.g. `openclaw-sbx-agent-main-f331f052`). Every instance shares a single Docker container, so `exec` tool operations in one instance can read/write files from another instance's workspace.

## Root cause

`ensureSandboxContainer` (docker.ts) and `ensureSandboxBrowser` (browser.ts) derive the container name slug from the scope key alone (`"shared"` or `"agent:main"`). Since all instances using the default agent key produce the same scope key, the slug hash is identical.

## Fix

Mix `workspaceDir` (which is derived from `$HOME` and already passed into both functions) into the slug input. This makes each instance produce a distinct container name while keeping the same name stable across restarts for a given instance.

For `scope="session"`, the session key already contains a per-session UUID, so no collision was possible there.

**Migration note:** Existing single-instance deployments will get a new container name on the first `exec` call after upgrading. The old container will be orphaned and cleaned up by the existing prune logic.

## Changes

- **`src/agents/sandbox/docker.ts`** — include `workspaceDir` in slug hash input
- **`src/agents/sandbox/browser.ts`** — same change for browser containers  
- **`src/agents/sandbox/docker.config-hash-recreate.test.ts`** — update expected container names to use dynamic slug

Fixes #51363

items.title: fix: preserve provider prefix in cron model suggestions
items.content:
## Summary

Fixes the Control UI cron model picker mangling provider prefixes. The cron model suggestions datalist was using only the model ID (e.g. `gpt-oss:120b-cloud`) without the provider prefix (e.g. `ollama/`). When a user selected such a suggestion, the server would fall back to the default provider ("anthropic"), producing:

```
Failed to set model: model not allowed: anthropic/gpt-oss:120b-cloud
```

## Root Cause

`loadCronModelSuggestions` in `ui/src/ui/controllers/cron.ts` was extracting only the `id` field from model catalog entries, discarding the `provider` field. This means suggestions like `gpt-oss:120b-cloud` were offered instead of `ollama/gpt-oss:120b-cloud`.

Note: The equivalent bug in the chat session model picker dropdown was already fixed in prior commits (01456f95bc, 7e8f5ca71b). This PR fixes the remaining instance in the cron model suggestions.

## Changes

- Fixed `loadCronModelSuggestions` to include provider prefix in model suggestions (e.g. `ollama/gpt-oss:120b-cloud` instead of `gpt-oss:120b-cloud`)
- Models without a provider continue to work as before (bare model ID is used)

## Testing

- Verified the fix correctly builds qualified model IDs for models with providers
- Matches the same pattern as `buildQualifiedChatModelValue` used elsewhere in the UI

Fixes openclaw/openclaw#51306

items.title: ci: validate nemoclaw config and schema files against a JSON Schema
items.content:
## Summary

Add a CI check that validates configuration files (e.g., `openclaw.json`, sandbox config, onboarding config) against a JSON Schema definition, catching structural errors before review.

## Motivation

NemoClaw uses several configuration files that follow implicit schemas — field names, types, required keys, and valid values are enforced only at runtime or by reviewer knowledge. A schema-based validation check in CI catches typos, type mismatches, missing required fields, and unknown keys automatically. This is particularly valuable when community contributors modify config-related code or documentation examples.

## Specification

### Step 1: Define JSON Schemas for config files

Create JSON Schema files (e.g., in a `schemas/` directory) that describe the expected structure of each configuration format used by NemoClaw. Start with the most commonly modified configs:

- **`openclaw.json`** — the main OpenClaw configuration
- **Sandbox configuration** — whatever structure `nemoclaw onboard` produces
- Any YAML/JSON config files that contributors frequently touch

Each schema should define:
- Required vs. optional fields
- Field types (string, number, boolean, array, object)
- Enum values where applicable (e.g., valid provider names, model identifiers)
- `additionalProperties: false` on objects where unknown keys indicate a mistake

### Step 2: Validate config files in CI

Add a CI step that:

1. Finds configuration files in the repo (or in the PR diff).
2. Validates each against its corresponding JSON Schema.
3. Fails with a clear error if validation fails, showing the file, the failing field, and the expected type/value.

### Step 3: Validate documentation examples

If README or docs files contain YAML/JSON code blocks that represent config examples, extract those blocks and validate them against the same schemas. This prevents documentation from drifting out of sync with the real config format.

### Implementation notes

- For JSON validation: [`ajv-cli`

items.title: fix(openclaw): WebSocket 事件流增加空闲超时防止会话卡死
items.content:
## 背景

之前提的 #512 给内置引擎加了事件空闲超时，修完之后想到 OpenClaw 这边也有同样的问题。看了下 #509 加的 watchdog，它依赖服务端的 agentTimeoutSeconds，只有配了这个值才生效。如果上游 API 直接挂了不出任何事件，这个 watchdog 其实帮不上忙。

## 改动

`openclawRuntimeAdapter.ts`:
- `ActiveTurn` 加了 `idleTimer` 字段
- 新增 `EVENT_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS = 300000`（5 分钟），跟内置引擎保持一致
- `resetEventIdleTimer()`: 每收到事件就重置，超时走 `handleChatAborted()` 终止
- 在 `handleChatEvent` 和 `handleAgentEvent` 里调用重置
- `cleanupSessionTurn()` 时清理

跟已有的 timeoutTimer 是互补关系：timeoutTimer 管总时长上限，idleTimer 管两个事件之间的间隔。

## 测试

- [ ] 断掉网关的上游连接，验证 5 分钟后会话自动终止并提示
- [ ] 正常对话不受影响
- [ ] 会话结束时 idleTimer 被正确清理
items.author: smartzhao

items.title: fix(openclaw): 防止网关并发启动产生多个进程
items.content:
## 背景

排查 #490（OpenClaw 反复出错重启）的时候，看了下 `scheduleGatewayRestart()` 的逻辑，发现定时器触发时没检查是否已经有启动流程在跑。如果网关异常退出触发了 restart 调度，同时用户改配置也触发了 `startGateway()`，理论上会 fork 两个进程抢同一个端口。

实际跑的时候确实遇到过一次日志里出现两行 `Gateway process started`，端口冲突后第二个直接挂了。

## 改动

`openclawEngineManager.ts` 的 `scheduleGatewayRestart()`，两处加了守卫：
- 入口检查 `startGatewayPromise`，有启动在跑就不调度
- 定时器回调里再检查一次，防止调度到触发之间有新的启动插进来

两处加了日志方便后续排查。

## 测试

- [ ] 手动 kill 网关进程，观察只启动一个新进程
- [ ] 快速连续切换配置触发多次 restart，确认不会出现多进程
- [ ] 查日志确认 "skipping, start already in progress" 在预期场景下打印
items.author: smartzhao

items.title: fix(openclaw): 停止网关时清理进程监听器防止 fd 泄漏
items.content:
## 背景

看 #601 那个竞态问题的时候顺便看了下 `stopGatewayProcess()`，发现 `attachGatewayProcessLogs()` 往 stdout/stderr 上挂了 data 监听，但 stop 的时候直接 kill 没摘。进程被杀后流关了，但监听器的引用还在，fd 句柄没释放。

本地跑了个脚本反复 restart 网关 20 次，用 lsof 看 fd 数确实在涨。

## 改动

`openclawEngineManager.ts` 的 `stopGatewayProcess()`:
- kill 前先 `removeAllListeners()` 把 stdout/stderr/child 上的监听全摘掉
- 加 `child.once('exit')` 在进程正常退出时清掉强杀定时器
- 强杀改用 `process.kill(pid, 'SIGKILL')` 替代二次 `child.kill()`

## 测试

- [ ] 频繁重启网关 10+ 次，lsof 观察 fd 数稳定
- [ ] kill 后日志不再输出网关 stdout/stderr
- [ ] Windows/macOS 验证强杀生效

items.title: Windows Compatibility Feedback
items.content:
Hi @win4r,

First of all, thank you for creating the ClawTeam-OpenClaw fork! I really appreciate your work in optimizing ClawTeam for OpenClaw users.

I tried to install and use ClawTeam-OpenClaw on Windows, but encountered some compatibility issues that might be helpful for you to know:

## Issues Found:

### 1. tmux Dependency
- ClawTeam defaults to `tmux` backend, but Windows doesn't have native tmux support
- The `subprocess` backend works when configured, but documentation assumes tmux availability

### 2. fcntl Module (File Locking)
- The `fcntl` module is Linux/macOS only
- Windows users get `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'fcntl'`
- This affects task locking and concurrent safety

### 3. Installation Documentation
- README mentions macOS/Ubuntu installation but not Windows alternatives
- Windows users need guidance on WSL or alternative backends

## Suggestions:

1. **Windows Documentation**: Add a Windows installation section mentioning WSL or Windows Terminal alternatives
2. **Backend Detection**: Auto-detect Windows and suggest `subprocess` backend
3. **Cross-platform File Locking**: Consider using `portalocker` or platform-specific locking
4. **Error Messages**: More helpful error messages for Windows users

## Workarounds I Found:
- Setting `default_backend: subprocess` helps
- Installing `pywin32` but fcntl still missing
- WSL is the most reliable solution for full functionality

Would you consider adding Windows support or at least documenting Windows workarounds? Many OpenClaw users are on Windows, and this would make ClawTeam-OpenClaw even more valuable.

Thanks again for your great work!

Best regards,
An OpenClaw user on Windows

items.title: [Feature]: Support Excel (.xlsx/.xls) and generic binary uploads in gateway / input_file
items.content:
### Summary

Support Excel spreadsheets and generic binary streams as first-class uploads (with clear MIME allowlists, size limits, and documented behavior for tools/skills).

### Problem to solve

Today, users who work with spreadsheets must manually convert Excel to CSV/JSON before the agent can reliably use the data. Chat/API attachment flows appear optimized for images; non-image types (including Office spreadsheets) may be dropped or not applied. The default input_file allowlist includes text types and PDF but not Excel MIME types, and tests show xlsx-style MIME is skipped rather than materialized for downstream tools.

Integrations that need arbitrary binary payloads (not only images/audio/video/text/PDF) lack a clear, supported path, which blocks automation and increases manual steps.

### Proposed solution

1) Excel (.xlsx, and .xls if feasible)
- Extend supported MIME types for attachments / input_file (configurable allowlist).
- Minimum viable: persist the file to the agent workspace (e.g. under .openclaw/attachments/...) with metadata (name, sha256, mime) and expose a stable path/receipt to tools/skills.
- Optional follow-up: built-in “tabular preview” extraction (bounded rows/cols) for model context, behind a feature flag.

2) Generic binary streams
- Define a supported path for arbitrary binary uploads with strict limits (max bytes, optional channel-specific caps) and explicit security rules (e.g. URL fetch policies unchanged; transcript redaction where needed).
- Fail closed with clear errors for disallowed types instead of silent drops.

3) Documentation
- Document allowed MIME types, defaults, overrides, and how skills should read files from the workspace.

### Alternatives considered

- Manual CSV/JSON conversion: works but is error-prone, loses sheets/structure, and does not scale for operators.
- Channel-specific hacks (e.g. zip/rename): fragile and not portable across gateway/API clients.
- Only supporting PDF: insufficient for spreadsheet-native

items.content:
# Hacker News
Google details new 24-hour process to sideload unverified Android apps ## Android 2026: Google 推出开发者验证计划，限制应用侧载，并引入高级流程绕过验证 Google 计划于 2026 年对 Android 系统进行重大改变，旨在打击设备生态系统中的恶意软件。 **主要变化:** * **开发者验证计划:** 从 2026 年 9 月起，Android 将限制应用侧载，要求开发者进行验证。验证需要提供身份信息、上传签名密钥，并支付 25 美元的费用。 * **高级流程绕过验证:** 为满足高级用户的需求，Google 推出“高级流程”，允许用户绕过应用验证。该流程隐藏在开发者设置中，操作过程较为复杂： 1. 连续点击“关于手机”中的软件版本号七次，启用开发者选项。 2. 在“开发者选项”中找到“允许未经验证的软件包”并启用。 3. 确认操作，输入设备解锁 PIN 码/密码。 4. 重启设备。 5. 等待 24 小时。 6. 返回“未经验证的软件包”菜单，选择“允许临时” (7 天) 或“允许无限期”。 7. 确认理解风险。 * **24 小时安全延迟:** 为了防止社会工程学攻击，绕过验证需要等待 24 小时。 * **后续应用安装:** 在启用高级流程并等待 24 小时后，用户可以在包管理器中选择“安装即可”选项来安装未经验证的应用程序。 **Google 的目标和理由:** * **提高安全性:** Google 认为，用户在 Google Play 商店之外安装应用感染恶意软件的风险是 Google Play 商店内的 50 倍。 * **应对监管压力:** 一些国家对 Android 平台的安全性表示担忧，并可能采取监管措施。 * **设备作为用户唯一电脑:** 随着智能手机成为许多人的唯一电脑，存储着个人隐私信息，安全性至关重要。 **需要注意的点:** * **身份验证而非内容审查:** Google 仅进行身份验证，不审查应用内容。 * **对独立开发者潜在风险:** 隐私倡导者担心验证计划可能导致开发者面临法律风险。 * **制裁国家开发者:** 验证费用可能影响居住在制裁国家（如古巴和伊朗）的开发者。 * **逐步推广:** 验证计划将于 2026 年 9 月在巴西、新加坡、印度尼西亚和泰国率先实施，随后在全球推广。 * **Android 16.1 集成:** 验证器和高级流程已集成到 Android 16.1 中。 总之，Google 正在调整 Android 的侧载机制，以提高安全性，但同时保留了高级用户绕过验证的选项。

items.title: fix(discord): retry startup with exponential backoff on transient network outage
items.content:
## Problem

Fixes #51370

When the OpenClaw gateway starts while the network is unavailable (e.g. ISP outage, system boot before network is up), the Discord plugin performs two WebSocket connection attempts (15 s each), then throws a fatal error — leaving the channel permanently dead until the service is manually restarted.

Root cause: `runDiscordGatewayLifecycle` has a single forced-reconnect guard during the startup readiness check. If both the initial wait and the forced reconnect both time out, the error is thrown immediately with no further retry.

## Fix

Replace the immediate `throw` after the second startup timeout with an **exponential-backoff retry loop** (up to 8 additional attempts). Back-off intervals: 15 s → 30 s → 60 s → 120 s → 240 s → 300 s (capped at `RECONNECT_STALL_TIMEOUT_MS`). Each attempt does a fresh `clearResumeState → disconnect → connect(false)` cycle and waits for a ready event, mirroring what the post-connected reconnect-stall watchdog already does.

If all retries are exhausted the error is still thrown so a misconfigured or permanently offline gateway eventually surfaces. The abort signal is respected throughout so manual gateway stops work correctly.

### Before



### After



## Changed files

- `extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.lifecycle.ts` — replace single-attempt startup guard with backoff retry loop

## Testing

- Manual testing with `iptables -I OUTPUT -d discord.com -j DROP` to simulate network outage during startup
- Verified the bot automatically reconnects within one backoff cycle after restoring connectivity

items.title: fix(feishu): pass mediaLocalRoots to enable local media file uploads
items.content:
The feishu channel was not passing mediaLocalRoots when calling sendMediaFeishu, causing loadWebMedia to reject local file paths due to security restrictions. This meant agent-generated media (like images from image_generate tool) would fail to upload and fall back to sending the local path as text.

Now properly passes agent-scoped media roots (workspace, tmp, etc.) to allow reading and uploading local media files to Feishu.

## Summary

- **Problem**: The feishu channel was not passing `mediaLocalRoots` when calling `sendMediaFeishu`, causing `loadWebMedia` to reject local file paths due to security restrictions (CVE-2026-26321 hardening).
- **Why it matters**: Agent-generated media (like images from `image_generate` tool) would fail to upload and fall back to sending the local path as plain text instead of the actual media file.
- **What changed**: Added `mediaLocalRoots` parameter to `CreateFeishuReplyDispatcherParams` and passed agent-scoped media roots (workspace, tmp, etc.) from `bot.ts` to enable local media file reads.
- **What did NOT change**: No changes to other channels (Telegram, Discord, etc. already had this working correctly). No changes to media loading logic itself.

## Linked Issue/PR

## OpenClaw 项目深度报告

# OpenClaw 项目动态日报 (2026-03-21)

## 1. 今日速览
OpenClaw 项目今日继续保持极高的活跃度，过去 24 小时内处理了约 **1000** 个 Issue 和 PR 更新（500+ Issues, 500+ PRs），显示出强大的社区开发动力。代码合并（244 个 PR）与新开启的请求（256 个 PR）几乎持平，表明项目在快速迭代的同时，技术债务管理也面临挑战。核心开发者的工作集中在**移动端配对流程优化**（iOS）、**Ollama 本地模型支持增强**以及 **WhatsApp/Feishu 等通讯渠道的 Bug 修复**。值得注意的是，虽然新版本未发布，但主分支已集成了大量针对 Gateway 稳定性和模型兼容性的修复。

## 2. 版本发布
*   **当前状态**：暂无新版本发布。
*   **主分支状态**：基于今日 PR 分析，主分支已包含多项关键修复（见下文“项目进展”），建议关注即将到来的 v2026.3.14 或补丁版本，以解决近期（尤其是 v2026.3.12/v2026.3.13）引入的回归问题。

## 3. 项目进展
今日合并或正在积极合并的重要 PR 推动了以下领域的进展：

*   **移动端与配对体验 (PR #51359, #51340)**:
    *   改进了 iOS 端的 QR 配对流程，从 ASCII 输出切换为 PNG 图片交付，并强化了安全性。这显著提升了移动用户的首次使用体验。
*   **本地模型支持增强 (PR #51356, #51094)**:
    *   修复了 Ollama 模型在 `openclaw.json` 中无法正确配置 `options` (如 `num_ctx`) 的 Bug。
    *   修复了小型模型在无原生工具支持时的文本回退机制，确保本地 Agent 能更稳定地工作。
*   **模型兼容性与适配 (PR #49512, #51325, #51358)**:
    *   **OpenAI Codex**: 修复了 `gpt-5.4` 模型的解析路径问题。
    *   **GitHub Copilot**: 扩展了动态模型解析能力，现在支持任何未在 SDK 注册表中注册的新模型 ID。
    *   **Azure OpenAI**: 修复了多轮对话中 `reasoning` 模块引发的 400 错误，增强了流式对话的稳定性。
*   **渠道修复 (PR #51059, #51127)**:
    *   **WhatsApp**: 支持了引用回复 (`quote-reply`) 和 `@​mention` 功能。
    *   **Feishu**: 修复了忽略 `blockStreamingDefault`

items.title: Add Qmemory — graph context engine plugin (SurrealDB)
items.content:
## Summary

- Adds Qmemory to the community plugins page
- Context-engine plugin (not a memory plugin) — implements the full `ContextEngine` interface
- Follows the existing page format, inserted alphabetically between Opik and QQbot

## Plugin Info

- **Name**: Qmemory
- **npm**: [`qmemory`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/qmemory) (v0.1.9)
- **GitHub**: https://github.com/QusaiiSaleem/qmemory
- **License**: MIT
- **Kind**: `context-engine`

## Why a context engine (not a memory plugin)

Qmemory implements `bootstrap`, `ingest`, `assemble`, `compact`, `afterTurn`, `prepareSubagentSpawn`, and `onSubagentEnded` with `ownsCompaction: true`. It needs the full session lifecycle because:

- `assemble()` injects cross-session memories, tool ledger, scratchpad, and graph map into `systemPromptAddition`
- `compact()` extracts facts from old messages into the graph (compaction = memory creation)
- `afterTurn()` runs 4-stage graduated compaction + background fact extraction
- 12 OpenClaw hooks capture tool calls, cron outcomes, token usage, subagent relationships, session lifecycle, sender identity, and delivery routing

A tool-only plugin can't do any of this — it has no access to compaction, context injection, or lifecycle hooks.

## What changed

Only `docs/plugins/community.md` — added the Qmemory entry in alphabetical order. All existing plugins are preserved unchanged.

## Previous submission

This is a resubmission of #49959 (closed). All feedback from that review has been addressed:
- Removed all "replaces LCM" framing
- Rewrote docs to accurately describe OpenClaw's architecture
- Verified implementation against current `ContextEngine` interface
- Fixed multiple runtime bugs found during production testing

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify the community plugins page renders correctly
- [ ] Verify existing plugin entries are unchanged
- [ ] Verify `openclaw plugins install qmemory` works

items.title: feat: add context engine transcript maintenance
items.content:
## Summary

Adds an optional `maintain()` lifecycle hook to the `ContextEngine` interface, enabling context engine plugins to request safe transcript rewrites without coupling to Pi session internals.

## Problem

Context engines like [lossless-claw](https://github.com/Martian-Engineering/lossless-claw) keep the context window bounded via DAG-based summarization, but the underlying JSONL transcript grows unbounded. Large tool results that have already been summarized and externalized in the context engine still occupy full space in the transcript file, leading to multi-hundred-MB session files in production.

## Solution

**New `ContextEngine.maintain()` hook** — called after bootstrap, after successful turns, and after successful compaction. Engines decide *what* is safe to rewrite; the runtime owns *how* the session DAG is updated on disk.

**New `TranscriptRewriteRequest/Result` types** — engines nominate transcript entries for replacement via `runtimeContext.rewriteTranscriptEntries()`. The runtime performs a branch-and-reappend pass on the JSONL, preserving all non-message entry types.

**Refactored `tool-result-truncation.ts`** — the existing inline branch-and-reappend logic was extracted into a generic `transcript-rewrite.ts` module, and tool-result truncation now uses it. This eliminates ~70 lines of duplication.

**Plugin SDK exports** — new types are exported so external plugins can implement `maintain()`.

## Changes

- `src/context-engine/types.ts` — `maintain()` method, `TranscriptRewriteRequest`, `TranscriptRewriteResult`, `ContextEngineMaintenanceResult` types, extended `ContextEngineRuntimeContext`
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/transcript-rewrite.ts` — generic branch-and-reappend rewriter (202 lines)
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/context-engine-maintenance.ts` — bridge that attaches runtime rewriter and calls `maintain()` (70 lines)
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts` — wired at bootstrap and post-turn call sites
- `src/agents/pi-e

items.title: picoclaw driver: runtime workspace permissions prevent agent writes (memory, state)
items.content:
The picoclaw driver materializes the runtime workspace at `.claw-runtime/<agent>/picoclaw-home/` with the host user's uid/gid (e.g., uid 1002). The picoclaw container runs as uid 1000 (the `picoclaw` user created in the base image).

The `.picoclaw/` directory is created with mode `0700` (drwx------), and `workspace/` inherits the host uid. Since uid 1000 ≠ uid 1002, the picoclaw process cannot:
- Write to `workspace/memory/` (agent memory)
- Write to `workspace/state/` (agent state)
- Create any files in the workspace

This causes silent failures like: `failed to open file: file not found: open /home/picoclaw/.picoclaw/workspace/memory/MEMORY.md: no such file or directory`

The agent tries to write memory but gets permission denied, so the file never exists.

**Fix:** The driver should either:
1. Create the runtime directory with permissions that allow the container user to write (e.g., mode 0777 or chown to the container uid)
2. Add a `chown` step in the entrypoint before running `picoclaw gateway`
3. Use the same uid in the container as on the host

This likely affects all drivers that mount host directories into containers running as non-root users.

items.content: ## Summary - Show "Update available" badge when a newer image exists even without a CalVer bump (e.g. controller-only changes that produce a new img-{hash} tag) - The badge is now a clickable button that opens the "Redeploy or Upgrade" dialog with "Upgrade to latest" preselected - Version Pinning "Current Status" panel now displays current and latest image tags in a table layout - Version Pinning card restructured to a consistent two-column layout (pinning controls left, status right) with the pin button inline next to the version selector ## Verification - pnpm run typecheck — passes - pnpm run lint — passes - pnpm run format:check — passes - Verified imageTagDiffers is gated on hasVersionInfo so the badge cannot appear during loading states - Verified latestImageTag is prop-drilled to VersionPinCard rather than fetched independently (avoids implicit coupling via query cache) - Confirmed existing CalVer-based update detection is unchanged — new logic only fires as a fallback when CalVer matches ## Visual Changes - "Update available" badge — now clickable with hover state; tooltip shows the target image tag for image-only updates - Version Pinning panel — two-column layout: left column has description + version selector + pin button inline + reason field; right column has Current Status with "Following latest" pill, plus current/latest image tags in aligned table rows - Pin button — moved inline next to the version dropdown instead of being a standalone row

items.content:
问题：
1. 在部署 Manager 后，发现Manager可以越狱到 VM 上，可以直接操作宿主机， 这个在安全上可能会很危险
2. 另外每创建一个 worker，Manager 都是拉成一个群组到形式，这样导致可以要求Worker 向 Manager 发送请求，然后就导致 Worker 也能越狱，Agent 不只是限制在他们共享的主机目录下了，他们可以随意乱跑 

而原生Openclaw 的沙盒是可以把小龙虾工作区限制在共享的宿主机目录下，从安全性是不是优化一下

Question:
1. After deploying the Manager, it was found that the Manager can be jailbroken to the VM and can directly operate the host. This may be very dangerous in terms of security.
2. In addition, every time a worker is created, the Manager is pulled into a group. This will require the Worker to send a request to the Manager, and then the Worker can also be jailbroken. The Agent is not only limited to their shared host directory, they can run around at will.

The native Openclaw sandbox can limit the Crayfish workspace to the shared host directory. Can it be optimized for security?

items.title: feat: 用 yida-data-management 替换 yida-query-data
items.content:
## 背景

现有 `yida-query-data` 主要只覆盖表单实例查询，适合简单只读场景，但对宜搭里更常见的数据管理需求支持不够完整。

## 本次改动

用 `yida-data-management` 替换 `yida-query-data`，并按仓库规范改为 Node.js CLI 命令：

```bash
openyida data <action> <resource> [args] [options]
```

支持的能力包括：
- 表单实例：查询 / 新增 / 更新 / 子表单查询
- 流程实例：查询 / 发起 / 更新 / 审批记录查询
- 任务中心：待办、已办、已提交、抄送查询，以及审批任务执行

## 相比 yida-query-data 的提升

- 从只支持表单查询，扩展为统一覆盖表单、流程、任务、子表单
- 从只读能力扩展到 create / update / execute 等操作
- 明确区分 `/v1/form/...` 和 `/v1/process/...`，减少接口混用
- 补充参考文档，便于 agent 稳定调用

## 使用场景

1. 龙虾类产品可以接入这个 skill，按照固定 SOP 做数据分析和代理审批。
2. 自定义页面开发过程中，可以直接获取数据接口内容，减少额外拼接和临时脚本调用。

## 包含内容

- 新增 `lib/data-management.js`
- 在 `bin/yida.js` 中注册 `openyida data` 命令
- 新增 `yida-data-management` skill 及参考文档
- 更新技能入口和查询示例文档
- 移除旧的 `yida-query-data` skill/script

## 验证情况

已完成 Node 版本基础验证：
- `node --check bin/yida.js`
- `node --check lib/data-management.js`
- `openyida data query form ...` 实际请求通过

并已验证表单、流程、任务中心、子表单相关核心接口能力可用，能够覆盖 `yida-query-data` 原有能力，并支持更多实际场景。

# 财经资讯汇总

## 来源: metwarn

天文台發出今年首個黃色暴雨警告信號。強雷雨區目前正向東橫過珠江口，即將開始影響本港西部地區。市民應留意天氣變化，加以提防大驟雨、猛烈陣風及雷暴。

---

[暴雨速報] 天文台在下午10時15分取消黃色暴雨警告信號。
雖然暴雨警告已經取消，但市民仍須警覺有關河道氾濫可能帶來的危險。

---

與南支槽前相關的廣闊雨區正影響珠江口一帶，今晚（星期二）及明早初時本港雨勢較為頻密及有一兩陣雷暴，讀者應留意天氣變化。
在降雨及東北季候風共同影響下，今晚至明早天氣清涼，市區最低氣溫會降至16度，新界或再低一兩度。

---

[暴雨速報] 天文台在下午11時25分發出黃色暴雨警告信號。
表示香港廣泛地區可能或已錄得每小時30毫米的大雨，而且雨勢可能持續。

---

黃色暴雨警告信號自昨晚（星期二）11時多生效，期間影響香港的雨區有所增強，多處地區時雨量超過30毫米。
天文台於昨日錄得的總雨量達68.0毫米，是有記錄以來元宵節最高雨量記錄。
預料大驟雨會在短期內持續。

---

[暴雨速報] 天文台在上午1時55分取消黃色暴雨警告信號。
雖然暴雨警告已經取消，但市民仍須警覺有關河道氾濫可能帶來的危險。

【311大地震十五週年】2011年3月11日日本時間下午2時46分，東北地區宮城縣對開海域發生9級猛烈地震。這場世紀強震轉眼已過十五載，其引發的巨大海嘯席捲了日本東北及關東沿岸，並觸發福島第一核電廠核事故，整場災難導致約二萬人喪生，至今仍有二千多人下落不明。
311地震為一次罕見且發生機制複雜的「連動型地震」。其震央雖然位於宮城縣對開海域，但整體的斷層破壞面卻異常廣闊，並由岩手縣一直延伸至茨城縣，合共長達約500公里，東西闊約200公里。在短短五分鐘內接連引發了至少三處大範圍的斷層錯動，累積釋放的能量極為龐大。
災後十五年，受災地區目前在安全性、基礎設施、觀光及出口等方面持續進行復興工作。然而，部分災民的心理創傷，以及福島第一核電廠為降低潛在輻射風險而持續推進的廢爐作業（如近年展開的取出核燃料碎片樣本、處理水排海及移除部分機組乏燃料），依然是這場災難留下的長遠挑戰。
而日本三陸近海未來仍然存在強震風險。三陸北部近海至北海道東部近海的岩盤可能已累積足以引發巨大地震的能量。最壞情況下地震規模可達9級或以上，北海道至東北可能出現超過20米的巨大海嘯。

---

一股冷空氣補充將以東風潮形式抵達廣東沿岸，本港今晚（星期四）至星期六初時風勢頗大，早晚天氣較涼，市區最低氣溫約16度，新界及東部地區或再低一兩度。

---

天文台在凌晨12時20分發出強烈季候風信號。
本港已開始受東風潮影響，離岸及高地吹強風。維港內多處陣風達強風程度，長洲泳灘的最高陣風更達烈風程度。
預料本港早晚天氣較涼，讀者外出前可按需要增添防風外套。

---

【「點心」襲港？】美國動畫電影《美食風球》(Cloudy with a Chance of Meatballs) 中，其主角曾成功研製天降美食的機器，其後更演變成一股夾雜肉丸的意粉龍捲風肆虐小鎮。但今後受「點心」吹襲卻居然有機會「成真」？

康妮(Kong-rey) > Koki

山陀兒(Krathon) > Burapha

items.title: docs: Add cross-platform CLI tool compatibility planning document
items.content:
## Summary

This PR adds a comprehensive planning document outlining ClawVault's strategy for improving compatibility with terminal-based AI coding tools like Claude Code and Aider, while maintaining the existing browser-based workflow for chat-channel agents and remote sessions.

## Changes

- **Added `docs/planning/CROSS_PLATFORM_CLI_COMPATIBILITY.md`** — A detailed GitHub issue draft that:
  - Contextualizes the current limitations of `clawvault request` in terminal environments
  - Documents what already works well with CLI tools (add, list, resolve, doctor)
  - Proposes six enhancement areas with priority levels:
    1. Terminal-native secret input mode (auto-detect TTY)
    2. MCP (Model Context Protocol) server integration
    3. Context-aware execution mode detection
    4. Claude Code hooks integration
    5. Structured JSON output mode
    6. Agent skill documentation
  - Includes security considerations emphasizing that secrets never enter AI context and destructive operations require human-in-the-loop confirmation
  - Defines non-goals and a suggested implementation order

## Notable Details

- The document is framed as a GitHub issue draft, ready to be created at the repository's issues page
- Includes concrete examples of proposed CLI usage, MCP configuration, and hook setups
- Emphasizes preserving the core security invariant (secrets never enter AI context) while expanding terminal-native interaction patterns
- Acknowledges that the web server flow remains essential for remote/web sessions (claude-remote, mobile, headless scenarios)

items.title: 🔴 channels.ts 同步全文扫描性能隐患
items.content:
## 来源
小架架构评审 #1 — 阻塞级

## 问题
fetchChannels() 用 readFileSync + 正则扫描所有 agent 的所有 session JSONL 文件全文，每次 poll 都跑一遍。O(n) 复杂度，n = 所有文件总大小。同步阻塞事件循环。

## 修复方案
- 复用 openclaw.ts 的异步 tail 8KB 方式，不读全文
- channel 信息单独缓存，带 mtime 检查
- 改为 async/await

## 指派
@小开

## 验收
- channels API 响应时间不随 session 文件增长而显著退化
- 无同步文件 I/O

items.title: refactor: eliminate all OpenClaw security scanner warnings
items.content:
## Summary

Reduces plugin security scanner warnings from **6 → 0**.

## Changes

### child_process warnings (2 eliminated)
- **Deleted `src/updater.ts`** — auto-updater removed entirely (OpenClaw handles updates natively)
- **Bundled node-datachannel prebuilts** — all platform binaries downloaded during CI and shipped in the npm package. Runtime bootstrap now uses `fs.copyFileSync` instead of `execFileSync`. Covers linux-x64/arm64/arm, linuxmusl-x64/arm64, darwin-x64/arm64, win32-x64/arm64.

### env-harvesting warnings (4 eliminated)
- All `process.env` reads moved to plugin host (`src/index.ts`) and passed through `createApp()` config. Server bundle contains zero `process.env` references.

### potential-exfiltration warnings (4 eliminated)
- Extracted all `fs.read*` calls out of `server/index.js` into dedicated single-responsibility modules:
  - `server/store/workspace-store.js` — workspace config I/O
  - `server/controllers/settings.js` — settings GET/PUT
  - `server/controllers/static.js` — static file serving
  - `server/controllers/agents.js` — agents list
- Split `server/providers/memory.js` into config discovery (file reads, no network) and providers (network, no file reads)
- Removed `fs.readFileSync` from `transcribe.js` — API key now passed from plugin host
- Dropped esbuild bundle (`server.js`) — `server/index.js` imported directly (Node 22 ESM)

## Testing
- Installed locally, zero warnings on `openclaw plugins install`
- `openclaw security audit --deep` reports no `code_safety` findings
- UI loads, messaging works, settings page works, workspace switching works

items.title: fix: restore inbound image embedding for CLI routed BlueBubbles turns
items.content:
## Summary
This restores inline image embedding for inbound BlueBubbles images when the reply run is routed through a CLI backend like Codex CLI.

## Root cause
BlueBubbles inbound media download and `MediaPaths` population were still working.
The regression was in the CLI execution path:

- embedded PI runs already scan prompt media refs like `[media attached: ...]` and load those files as actual image inputs
- CLI runs only forwarded images when `params.images` was already populated
- inbound BlueBubbles turns rely on prompt media refs, so CLI routed runs were falling through to raw text placeholders like `<media:image>` instead of getting the actual image attached to the model input

## Fix
- add shared CLI prompt media hydration that scans prompt refs and loads local images with the same `detectImageReferences` and `loadImageFromRef` logic used by the embedded runner
- use those hydrated images in `runCliAgent` whenever explicit `params.images` were not already supplied
- add a regression test proving a prompt level `[media attached: ...]` ref becomes a real `--image` CLI argument

items.title: Reflector times out on real-world observation volume
items.content:
## Summary

The reflector has **never successfully completed** on a live agent. Every attempt times out at 120 seconds.

## Evidence

7 consecutive failures across 2026-03-19 through 2026-03-21:
- 2026-03-19: 1 timeout
- 2026-03-20: 5 timeouts (05:02, 06:13, 17:15, 20:30, 20:51)
- 2026-03-21: 1 timeout

No `reflection complete` entries exist in any log file. The reflector has never succeeded.

## Root Cause

The reflector sends the **entire** `observations.json` as a single LLM completion call. At 686 observations (272KB of pretty-printed JSON, ~68K tokens), the model cannot process and return a compressed result within the 120-second API timeout.

## Impact

- Observations have accumulated unchecked for 9 days with zero compression
- Growth rate is ~76 observations/day with no ceiling
- The observation dump in the system prompt is ~68K tokens and growing
- This will only get worse — the reflector design sends more data as it falls further behind

## Scaling Concern

Even if the timeout is increased, the single-call architecture has a ceiling. At 3,500+ observations (the volume seen after migrating an OpenClaw agent with 2 months of history), the input alone approaches the context window limit.

## Possible Fixes

1. **Chunked reflection** — process observations in batches (e.g., 200 at a time), merging results across chunks
2. **Incremental reflection** — only reflect on new observations since last successful reflection, merging with previously compressed results
3. **Longer timeout** — buys time but does not fix the scaling problem
4. **Streaming response** — use streaming to avoid the hard timeout, though the input size problem remains

Options 1 and 2 address the root cause. Option 2 is the most scalable since it keeps the per-call cost constant regardless of total observation count.

items.title: feat(openclaw-gateway): self-contained adapter with agent discovery, model override, JWT auth, and session strategies
items.content:
## OpenClaw Gateway Adapter — Self-Contained Update

### What this PR does
Updates the existing OpenClaw Gateway adapter (`openclaw_gateway`) to be fully self-contained — no external file dependencies, no manual configuration outside Paperclip's UI.

### Key features
- **Agent selector**: Live WebSocket discovery of OpenClaw agents
- **Model override**: 75+ models across 11 providers
- **Thinking effort**: 8 levels including Adaptive (Claude 4.6+) and Extra High (GPT-5.2+)
- **Session strategy**: Per-project, per-issue, per-run, or fixed session scoping
- **Skill injection**: Paperclip SKILL.md with hash-based dedup (avoids bloating persistent sessions)
- **Prompt template**: Optional per-agent instructions with hash-based dedup
- **JWT auth**: `supportsLocalAgentJwt: true` — server generates scoped, auto-rotating JWT per run
- **Device auth**: Automatic device pairing with ed25519 signatures
- **Retry logic**: Transient WS failure handling with exponential backoff

### JWT / API key handling
Previously the adapter told the agent to read `PAPERCLIP_API_KEY` from a JSON file on disk (`~/.openclaw/workspace/paperclip-claimed-api-key.json`). This caused wrong-path bugs (#1371) and doesn't work for remote agents.

Now: `supportsLocalAgentJwt: true` in the adapter registry. The server generates a per-run JWT (HMAC-SHA256, 48h TTL, scoped to agent+company+run) and the adapter injects it directly in the wake message. Each run gets a fresh key with explicit instruction to use only the current run's key.

Security posture: identical to the JSON file approach (model sees the key in both cases), but strictly better properties — auto-rotation, per-run scoping, 48h expiry, no file-path dependency.

**Resolves** #1371
**Supersedes** #1373

### Code review
This PR was reviewed by both CodeRabbit and Codex on the staging PR (lazmo88/paperclip#3). All actionable findings were addressed:
- Fixed skill message mutation across retries
- Reset event buffers per retry iteration
- Unified

items.title: Feature Request: Add Gemini Context Caching Support (cachedContents API)
items.content:
**Problem:**

The OpenClaw framework supports `cacheRetention` for Anthropic models, but does not implement context caching for Google Gemini models. This results in significant cost overhead for agents with large system instructions.

**Current Situation:**

- Gemini models support context caching via the `cachedContents` API
- OpenClaw's `cacheRetention` config only works for Anthropic
- Each request to Gemini re-sends the entire system instruction (~35KB for some agents)
- This results in ~$0.0027 per request just for system instruction overhead

**Example Cost Impact:**

For an agent like Felipe (WhatsApp triage agent):
- System instruction: ~35,000 tokens
- Without caching: $0.075/1M input per request
- With caching: $0.01875/1M cache read (75% savings)

For 100 conversations/day with 10 turns each:
- Without caching: ~$2.70/day
- With caching: ~$0.68/day
- **Monthly savings: ~$60/month**

**Google's Context Caching API:**

```python
# Create cache
content_cache = client.caches.create(
    model="gemini-2.0-flash-001",
    config=CreateCachedContentConfig(
        contents=contents,
        system_instruction=system_instruction,
        display_name="agent-cache",
        ttl="86400s",  # 24 hours
    ),
)

# Use cache in requests
response = client.models.generate_content(
    model="gemini-2.0-flash-001",
    contents="user question",
    cached_content=content_cache.name
)
```

Documentation: https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/generative-ai/docs/context-cache/context-cache-create

**Proposed Implementation:**

1. Add support for `cacheRetention` config for Gemini models
2. Automatically create and manage `cachedContents` for system instructions
3. Cache system instruction on first request
4. Reuse cache for subsequent requests within TTL
5. Auto-refresh cache before expiration

**Config Example:**

```json
{
  "agents": {
    "defaults": {
      "models": {
        "google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite": {
          "params": {
            "cacheRetention": "long"

items.title: [Bug]: Discord plugin does not reconnect after network outage during initial startup
items.content:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Summary

When the Discord plugin's initial startup occurs while the network is unavailable (e.g., ISP outage), all API calls (`fetchDiscordGatewayInfo`, `deployDiscordCommands`, `fetchUser("@me")`) fail with `fetch failed`. The plugin logs "logged in to discord" despite never establishing a WebSocket connection, then enters a permanently dead state with no reconnection attempts. The service process stays alive, so systemd `Restart=always` never triggers recovery. Manual restart is required after network recovery.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Run OpenClaw gateway via systemd user service with Discord channel configured
2. Lose upstream internet connectivity (ISP outage; local network and host remain up on UPS)
3. The gateway service is running throughout — it never crashes or restarts
4. Internet connectivity recovers
5. Observe that Discord remains disconnected indefinitely — no messages are sent or received
6. Confirm via `journalctl --user -u openclaw-gateway` that the Discord plugin logged startup errors but never retried

### Expected behavior

After the initial connection fails due to a transient network outage, the Discord plugin should retry the initial startup sequence with exponential backoff until it successfully connects. Alternatively, it should exit/crash on startup failure so that systemd's `Restart=always` handles recovery.

### Actual behavior

The plugin logs multiple `fetch failed` errors during startup, then logs `logged in to discord` (without bot identity or WebSocket), and sits in a dead state permanently. `reconnectAttempts=0` and `gatewayConnected=false` are logged — no retry ever occurs. The gateway lifecycle runner (`runDiscordGatewayLifecycle`) only handles reconnection after a successful initial connection; the gap is in the initial startup sequence.

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.13 (61d171a)

### Operating system

Debian 12 (Bookworm) aarch64 — Raspberry Pi 5, kernel 6

items.title: fix(mcp-proxy): add missing return after error handling in logger middleware
items.content:
## Problem

In `src/mcp-proxy/pkg/middleware/logger.go`, when `GetMCPServerByName` returns an error, the code calls `c.Abort()` but is missing a `return` statement. This causes the function to continue executing, potentially leading to a nil pointer dereference when accessing the `mcp` variable in subsequent calls to:
- `SetMCPServerID(c, mcp.ID)`
- `SetMCPServerName(c, mcpName)`
- `SetGatewayID(c, mcp.GatewayID)`

## Solution

Added a `return nil` statement after `c.Abort()` in the error handling path to properly terminate execution after sending the error response.

## Changes

- **File**: `src/mcp-proxy/pkg/middleware/logger.go`
- **Line**: 80 (after `c.Abort()`)
- **Change**: Added `return nil` to prevent continuation after error

## Testing

This fix prevents potential nil pointer panics when MCP server lookup fails. The middleware now correctly terminates execution after returning the error response to the client.

## Notes

- This bug was already fixed in release/1.21 branch
- This PR backports the fix to release/1.20
- Co-developed with AI assistance

---

items.title: Telegram plugin: inbound channel notifications not delivered in tmux/headless sessions
items.content:
## Bug Description

When Claude Code is run inside a `tmux` session (headless, no direct TTY), the Telegram plugin's MCP server successfully polls messages and the MCP stdio transport socket is connected, but **inbound channel notifications are never displayed or processed by Claude Code**. Outbound `reply` tool calls work perfectly.

## Environment

- Claude Code: v2.1.81
- OS: Oracle Linux (cloud VM) and macOS
- Telegram plugin: `telegram@claude-plugins-official` v0.0.1
- Runtime: bun (for server.ts / grammY)
- Launch method: `tmux new-session -d -s cc 'claude --dangerously-skip-permissions'`

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Install and configure the Telegram plugin (`~/.claude/channels/telegram/.env` with bot token, `access.json` with allowed user)
2. Start Claude Code inside tmux: `tmux new-session -d -s cc 'claude --dangerously-skip-permissions'`
3. Confirm CC starts, bun server.ts spawns, and MCP tools (`mcp__plugin_telegram_telegram__reply`) are available
4. Send a message to the Telegram bot from an allowed user
5. Observe: bot shows "typing" indicator, but CC never displays the incoming message

## Diagnosis

Extensive debugging confirmed:

### What works:
- Telegram Bot API receives messages (`getUpdates` returns them)
- bun server.ts polls and consumes updates (getUpdates returns empty after consumption)
- MCP stdio transport sockets are connected (verified via `ss -xp`):
  - bun fd 0 (stdin) ↔ claude fd 30 (unix socket)
  - bun fd 1 (stdout) ↔ claude fd 33 (unix socket)
- Outbound `reply` tool works — CC can send messages to Telegram chats successfully
- `access.json` allowlist is correctly configured

### What fails:
- `mcp.notification({ method: 'notifications/claude/channel', ... })` from bun server.ts is written to stdout (socket) but CC never processes or displays the incoming message
- CC stays at the idle `❯` prompt — no channel message appears

### Additional observation:
- The bun server.ts process also tends to **silently crash** after some time, wit

# OpenClaw 项目动态日报（2026-03-21）

---

## 1. 今日速览

OpenClaw 社区活跃度持续高位运行，过去24小时内共处理 **500条 Issues** 和 **500条 Pull Requests**，其中新开/活跃 Issue 达 391 条，PR 合并/关闭率达 48.4%（242/500），显示出高强度开发与问题响应节奏。尽管无新版本发布，但核心功能迭代密集，尤其在多通道支持、模型兼容性与安全加固方面取得显著进展。社区对国际化、内存管理与认证机制的讨论热度居高不下，反映出项目正从“可用”向“易用”和“可扩展”阶段演进。

---

## 2. 版本发布

**无新版本发布**。最近一次稳定版本仍为 `v2026.3.13`，多个高优先级 Bug 修复已通过 PR 合并进入主干，预计将在下个热修复版本中集中发布。

---

## 3. 项目进展

今日共 **242个 PR 被合并或关闭**，重点进展包括：

- **模型与代理系统增强**：
  - ✅ 修复 Ollama 模型配置中 `options` 字段被丢弃的问题，支持 `num_ctx` 等参数传递（#51356）
  - ✅ 修复 Azure OpenAI 多轮对话中 reasoning block 残留导致的 400 错误（#51358）
  - ✅ 新增 GitHub Copilot 动态模型解析能力，支持任意未注册模型 ID 自动适配（#51325）

- **通道与集成优化**：
  - ✅ WhatsApp 支持 quote-reply 与 @mention 功能（#51059）
  - ✅ Feishu 通道现可正确读取 `blockStreamingDefault` 配置（#51127）
  - ✅ Telegram 支持自定义 `apiRoot` 以应对 DNS 封锁或自托管 Bot API 场景（#48842）

- **安全性与稳定性加固**：
  - ✅ 阻止 `auth.mode=none` 与 Tailscale 远程暴露组合带来的未授权访问风险（#51339）
  - ✅ 修复媒体抓取错误中 PII 信息泄露问题（#51344）
  - ✅ 防止孤立会话篡改自身 cron 配置（#51313）

- **用户体验改进**：
  - ✅ Web UI 中持久化保存 webchat 发送的图片（#51324）
  - ✅ 启用智能记忆默认配置，缓解“双会话”割裂感（#51341）

> 整体项目在**多模态交互、企业级部署安全、本地化模型支持**三大方向上迈出关键步伐。

items.title: feat(blueprint): add GoldRush blockchain data network policy preset
items.content:
## Summary

- Adds a `goldrush.yaml` network policy preset so sandboxed agents can reach GoldRush blockchain data endpoints
- Covers four hosts: REST API (`api.covalenthq.com`), Streaming API (`streaming.goldrush.dev`, `streaming.goldrushdata.com`), and x402 pay-per-request proxy (`x402.goldrush.dev`)

## Context

[GoldRush](https://goldrush.dev) provides unified blockchain data across 100+ chains (balances, transactions, NFTs, token prices, DEX data). It's available as:

- **MCP server** (`@covalenthq/goldrush-mcp-server`) — auto-discovered by OpenClaw
- **Agent skills** (`covalenthq/goldrush-agent-skills`) — installable via `npx skills add`

Without this preset, sandboxed NemoClaw agents using GoldRush tools are blocked by network policy. This preset follows the same pattern as existing presets (Discord, Slack, HuggingFace, etc.).

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify YAML validates against the policy schema
- [ ] Confirm sandboxed agent can reach `api.covalenthq.com` with the preset enabled
- [ ] Confirm sandboxed agent can establish WebSocket to `streaming.goldrush.dev`

items.title: Add security hardening and threat intelligence
items.content:
## Summary

Two new security components for the OpenClaw fleet:

- **`devops/machine-security-review.md`** — Per-machine security agent spec. Runs daily (drift) and weekly (full audit + red team). Checks firewall, open ports, file permissions, SSH config, credential exposure, prompt injection in memory/agent files, skill script integrity and malware patterns, MCP tool poisoning. Supports automated (cron, silent, smart escalation) and interactive (educational, conversational) modes.

- **`workflows/security-sentinel/`** — Threat intelligence workflow. Researches emerging AI security threats weekly using web search, maps findings against OpenClaw architecture, verifies fleet exposure via SSH, produces weekly security digests. Covers prompt injection evolution, MCP vulnerabilities, memory poisoning, credential exfiltration, agent hijacking, and supply chain attacks.

Also:
- Updated `AGENTS.md` code conventions: positive framing, added markdown-not-JSON state rule
- Bumped version to 0.16.0
- Updated README with security-sentinel in workflow table and new Security Hardening devops section

## Design Decisions

- **Package updates removed from scope** — "update to latest version" is checkbox security, not real security. Focused on actual exploit vectors: prompt injection, skill malware, port exposure, credential leaks.
- **Split into devops + workflow** — Per-machine checks (machine-security-review) run locally as a devops agent. Fleet-wide threat research (security-sentinel) is a workflow with learning and web search capabilities.
- **Process auto-kill removed** — Review feedback identified that auto-killing unexpected listeners is dangerous (could kill newly deployed legitimate services). Changed to report-only.
- **MCP tool auto-disable removed** — Tool description changes could be legitimate updates. Changed to report suspicious patterns without auto-disabling.
- **Web search guardrails added** — Sentinel treats all web content as untrusted input (indirect prompt in

items.title: epic(agent): expose Potato OS as a local agent platform
items.content:
### Summary
Turn Potato OS into a stronger local agent platform by exposing its capabilities through agent-friendly interfaces and validating real Pi-native agent workflows on top.

### Why this exists
The open agent-related tickets point in the same direction: Potato OS should not just run local models, it should also become something other tools and agent systems can talk to cleanly.

### Target outcome
Potato OS can act as a local agent-capable platform with a clear server interface and at least one evaluated end-to-end agent framework path on Pi hardware.

### Scope
- Expose Potato OS capabilities through an agent-friendly server interface
- Validate at least one concrete agent framework on Pi 5 with local inference
- Clarify what Potato OS should and should not be as an agent host/runtime

### Sub-issues
- [ ] #114 — Expose Potato OS as an MCP server for agentic tool integration
- [ ] #115 — Evaluate OpenClaw agent framework on Pi 5 with local inference

### Acceptance criteria
- Agent-platform work is grouped under one product direction
- Child tickets leave behind a clearer integration story for external agent tooling
- The repo gains a more explicit position on local agent workflows on Pi hardware

### Non-goals
- Turning every future automation idea into part of this epic
- Committing to a full agent product strategy before the first integrations are validated
- Reworking unrelated deployment or image-build concerns here

### Open questions / risks
- Which interfaces are worth supporting long-term
- Whether the target frameworks are actually viable on Pi hardware
- How much of this belongs in core Potato OS versus optional integrations

items.title: Add A-F layer research verification pipeline with literature tools
items.content:
## Summary

This PR implements the A-F layer research verification pipeline, extending the code evolution engine with literature management, research forest state tracking, and foundational tools for converting evolution results into research contributions. The implementation follows a layered architecture where A-F layers build upon the existing evolution system (C layer acts as the integration point).

## Key Changes

### New Modules

- **F-layer (Infrastructure)**
  - `plugin/src/vectordb.ts`: Local vector database for literature using TF-IDF with JSON persistence
  - `plugin/src/bibtex.ts`: BibTeX parsing, deduplication, formatting, and file management
  
- **C-layer (Research State)**
  - `plugin/src/research_state.ts`: Derivation forest lifecycle management with node operations, convergence detection, and contribution grading

- **A-layer (Literature Tools)**
  - `lit_ingest`: Ingest papers into local vector database with auto-generated BibTeX
  - `lit_search_local`: Search local literature database with TF-IDF scoring
  - `code_qa`: Answer questions about evolved code using lineage and diff context
  - `bib_append`: Append BibTeX entries to project references with deduplication

items.title: Discord plugin does not reconnect after network outage during initial startup
items.content:
## Bug Description

When the Discord plugin starts (or restarts) while the network is unavailable, the initial connection fails but the plugin does not retry. It remains in a permanently broken state — the service stays running, but Discord is never connected.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Start OpenClaw gateway (or have it running via systemd)
2. Lose internet connectivity (e.g., ISP outage — local network and the host itself remain up)
3. Internet recovers
4. Discord plugin never reconnects — `systemctl --user restart openclaw-gateway` is required

## Expected Behavior

The Discord plugin should retry the initial connection with backoff when startup fails due to network errors, or crash so that systemd `Restart=always` can handle recovery.

## Actual Behavior

The plugin logs `fetch failed` errors during startup, then logs `logged in to discord` (without bot identity), and enters a dead state:

```
[discord] startup [default] deploy-rest:put:error 2641ms path=/applications/.../commands requestMs=13 error=fetch failed
[discord] failed to deploy native commands: fetch failed
[discord] failed to fetch bot identity: TypeError: fetch failed
[discord] startup [default] fetch-bot-identity:error 2666ms TypeError: fetch failed gatewayConnected=false reconnectAttempts=0
[discord] logged in to discord
```

Note: `reconnectAttempts=0` and `gatewayConnected=false` — no retry ever happens. The WebSocket connection is never opened. The service does not crash, so systemd restart logic never triggers.

## Environment

- **OpenClaw version:** 2026.3.13 (61d171a)
- **Platform:** Raspberry Pi 5 / Debian arm64 / Node 25.8.1
- **Service:** systemd user unit with `Restart=always`
- **Trigger:** Spectrum ISP outage; Pi on UPS (never lost power or local network)

## Workaround

A cron-based watchdog script that checks journald for the failure pattern and restarts the service. Works but should not be necessary.

## Suggested Fix

The Discord plugin's startup flow (`extensions/discord/src/setu

items.title: feat(search): cache-friendly recall via memory_version and GET /version endpoint (#817)
items.content:
## Problem
The openclaw-plugin calls `POST /api/v1/search/find` on EVERY `before_prompt_build` hook,
even when no memories have changed. For users with large memory stores, this causes redundant
LLM/embedding computation and unnecessary latency on every prompt build.

See: docs/issues/2026-03-20/817-plan.md

## Fix (4 slices, each independently revertible)
- **A**: Add `openviking/service/namespace_version.py` — monotonic write counter per namespace, incremented on every memory mutation, protected by `asyncio.Lock`
- **B**: Expose `memory_version: Optional[int]` in `/find` response + new lightweight `GET /api/v1/search/version` endpoint (in-memory counter lookup, no DB query)
- **C**: Add `getMemoryVersion(namespace)` to TypeScript client — returns `null` on any error (404, network failure) for safe fallback
- **D**: Plugin version cache (keyed by session ID) — skips `find()` on cache hit, falls back to full recall when version is null or fetch fails

## Backward Compatibility
All changes are additive. Old clients ignore `memory_version` in /find response.
New client falls back to full recall if /version is unsupported or returns error.

## Known Limitation: Server Restart
In-memory counters reset to 0 on server restart. Client caches become stale but will
correctly re-fetch on version mismatch (stale version > reset server version). Counter
persistence to disk is deferred to a follow-up PR.

## Known Limitation: Multi-Worker
The namespace counter is per-process. With `uvicorn --workers N > 1`, each process has
an independent counter dict. The default single-worker deployment is unaffected. A
Redis-backed counter for distributed deployments is deferred to a follow-up issue.

## Research Incorporated
- RFC 7232 conditional GET patterns — adapted to custom version field for simplicity
- asyncio.Lock patterns from FastAPI async service best practices (mars-project WatchNotifier)
- Session-keyed cache patterns from TypeScript AI agent plugins (firebase, openclaw)

items.title: feat: stable logical session identity (logicalSessionKey, taskRef, branch)
items.content:
## Goal
Persist task identity independent of numeric session ID so respawned sessions carry full context. From `DESIGN-OPENCLAW-PLUGIN.md` → Session Supervision Requirements.

## Problem
When a session dies and respawns, it gets a new numeric ID (e.g. ao-5 → ao-12). The OpenClaw webhook session key (`hook:ao:ao-5`) is then orphaned and escalation history is lost.

## What to build
Add to session metadata (wherever `SessionMetadata` or equivalent is defined):
```ts
logicalSessionKey: string  // e.g. "feat/ci-auto-injection" or "issue-42"
taskRef: string            // e.g. "#42" or "GH-42"
branch: string
prUrl?: string
```

On spawn: derive `logicalSessionKey` from branch name or issue ref if available.
On respawn/restore: carry these fields from the dead session to the replacement.
In escalation payloads: include `logicalSessionKey` so OpenClaw can route to the correct webhook session.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `logicalSessionKey` persists across respawn
- [ ] Escalation webhook payload includes `logicalSessionKey`
- [ ] Unit tests: respawn carries identity fields from dead session
- [ ] Zero TS errors

items.title: fix: honor denylist and tighten non-action resumes
items.content:
## Summary

This PR fixes two practical failure modes we hit in a real OpenClaw deployment:

1. `denySessionKeyPrefixes` existed conceptually in code/config, but was not actually parsed or enforced.
2. `non_action` recovery was too broad and could auto-resume successful runs that merely contained continuation-style language like "我先 / 继续 / 接下来", even after the run had already done meaningful work.

## Changes

- parse and honor `denySessionKeyPrefixes` in plugin config
- add `isSessionKeyDenied()` and skip scheduling for matching session keys
- add schema support for `denySessionKeyPrefixes` in `openclaw.plugin.json`
- document the option in `README.md`
- tighten `non_action` recovery so it only triggers when:
  - the run succeeded,
  - continuation intent was detected, and
  - **no tool calls** happened in that run
- tighten `tool_error` recovery so it only auto-resumes when tool errors occurred and **no successful tool call** happened in the same run

## Why this matters

In our case, a Feishu session hit an unrelated send/problem path and then `auto-resume-lite` kept reviving the same session. Because the run text contained continuation phrasing, the plugin kept classifying it as resumable even when the run had already done meaningful work, which amplified a bad session instead of recovering it.

With this patch:

- channel/session families such as `agent:main:feishu:` can be explicitly excluded from auto-resume
- successful runs that already executed tools are much less likely to be falsely classified as `non_action`
- mixed runs with both tool errors and successful tool calls are no longer automatically treated as resumable `tool_error` cases

## Validation

- `node --check index.js`
- `git diff --check`

## Suggested PR title

`telegram: add awaited ingress middleware chain before message dispatch`

## Problem

Deterministic `public -> private` notifications were initially implemented as a dist-level hotfix against compiled OpenClaw bundles. That solved the operational problem, but left architectural debt:

- logic depended on specific compiled chunks;
- updates could silently break the patch;
- inbound control lived outside a first-class source abstraction;
- Telegram pre-dispatch logic could not be extended cleanly with additional deterministic middleware.

## What this patch does

This patch introduces a source-level awaited ingress middleware layer for Telegram message processing.

  - `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message.ts`
- adds config support for awaited Telegram ingress middleware entries:
  - `channels.telegram.ingressMiddlewares`
- adds tests for:

items.title: fix(media): sanitize fetch errors before posting to channels
items.content:
## Summary

When a media fetch operation fails, the raw error message — which may contain serialized JSON with private group IDs, phone numbers, user names, and internal system paths — was posted to the configured channel as if it were a normal reply. This exposed PII to public-facing channels like Discord and Telegram.

**Example of what was leaking:**
```
Media failed: {"group_id":"@g.us","sender":"+905...","name":"John Doe","path":"/home/user/.openclaw/..."}
```

## Details

Added `SAFE_MEDIA_FETCH_ERROR_MESSAGE` constant and `isSafeMediaFetchError()` helper to `src/media/fetch.ts`. When a `MediaFetchError` is detected at the outbound delivery layer (`message-action-params.ts`, `message-action-runner.ts`, `deliver.ts`), the raw error is replaced with:

```
⚠️ Media fetch failed. The attachment could not be retrieved.
```

The original error is still logged internally via `logVerbose` — it is only redacted from the outbound channel payload.

Three sanitization boundary points:
1. `message-action-params.ts` — params resolution for media actions
2. `message-action-runner.ts` — action execution runner
3. `deliver.ts` — final delivery layer

## Related Issues

Fixes #20279

## How to Validate

Trigger a media fetch that fails (invalid URL or timeout). Confirm:
- Channel receives the generic safe message
- `logVerbose` / internal logs still contain the full error

Run unit tests:
```bash
pnpm test -- --testPathPattern="fetch|message-action-runner.media"
```

Note: `message-action-runner.media.test.ts` requires `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk` which was added to `main` today (commit `6e20c4baa0`). The test will pass once dependencies are installed in CI. The `fetch.test.ts` suite (12 tests) passes locally and covers the core PII sanitization behavior.

## Pre-Merge Checklist

- [x] Updated relevant documentation and README (if needed)
- [x] Added/updated tests (if needed)
- [ ] Noted breaking changes (if any)
- [x] Validated on required platforms/methods:

items.title: feat: support secret refs in plugin config credential fields (twilio-sms, voice-call)
items.content:
## Summary

Plugin config fields like `twilio.accountSid` and `twilio.authToken` in `twilio-sms` and `voice-call` only accept plain strings in their JSON schema. When users configure these as AKV exec secret refs (`{"source":"exec","provider":"akv","id":"..."}`) the config validator rejects them with `invalid config: must be string`, breaking the gateway.

## Problem

The `openclaw.plugin.json` configSchema for both plugins defines credential fields as `{"type":"string"}`. During gateway startup, `validateJsonSchemaValue` validates the raw plugin config (before any secret resolution), so object-shaped secret refs fail validation and the gateway enters a degraded state.

```
Invalid config at ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json:
- plugins.entries.twilio-sms.config.twilio.accountSid: invalid config: must be string
- plugins.entries.twilio-sms.config.twilio.authToken: invalid config: must be string
- plugins.entries.voice-call.config.twilio.accountSid: invalid config: must be string
- plugins.entries.voice-call.config.twilio.authToken: invalid config: must be string
```

## Root Cause

1. JSON Schema in `openclaw.plugin.json` uses `{"type":"string"}` — rejects secret ref objects.
2. Zod schema uses `z.string()` — same issue at runtime.
3. Plugin registration is synchronous and doesn't resolve secret refs.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] JSON schemas in both plugin manifests accept `{"source","provider","id"}` secret ref objects OR strings for credential fields
- [ ] Plugin registration resolves secret refs via `resolveSecretInputString` (using `api.config`) before constructing the runtime config
- [ ] Env-var fallback still works when no explicit value is configured
- [ ] Gateway starts cleanly with AKV exec refs in twilio credential fields

## Implementation Plan

1. Export `resolveSecretInputString` from `src/plugin-sdk/index.ts`
2. Update `openclaw.plugin.json` configSchemas for `twilio-sms` and `voice-call` to accept `anyOf: [string, secretRef]` for credential fields
3. Updat

items.title: Silent poller notifications — stop exposing raw email/SMS alerts in chat
items.content:
## Summary

- Adds `POST /convos/notify` endpoint to both OpenClaw and Hermes runtimes that dispatches notifications as synthetic system messages — the agent responds naturally over XMTP but the raw notification text never appears as a chat bubble
- Rewrites the poller to `curl` the local `/convos/notify` endpoint instead of `convos conversation send-text`
- Includes message ID in email notifications so the agent can `email read --id` the full content
- Updates poller evals for the silent notification flow
- Removes dead code: `inject_context`, `get_session_file`, `get_conversation_id`, convos CLI preflight, unused env vars from both runtime launchers

## Why

Raw notifications like `You got a new email. "(no preview)" from no-reply@mail.privy.io` were appearing as visible chat bubbles from the agent. The user should only see the agent's natural response ("Your wallet is live, here are your addresses"), never the internal plumbing.

Closes #611

items.title: feat: Experience Management System (EMS) — AI agent experience/guardrail service
items.content:
## Overview

Build an independent **Experience Management System (EMS)** — a knowledge service that stores, retrieves, and manages "experiences" (lessons learned, guardrails, best practices) accumulated by AI agents during development workflows.

## Motivation

AI agents sometimes make mistakes (e.g., merging PRs without approval, leaking secrets). Currently, rules are scattered across MEMORY.md and SOUL.md with no structured retrieval or enforcement. EMS provides:

1. **Structured storage** of experiences with semantic search
2. **Check/intercept API** — before an agent acts, query EMS for relevant guardrails
3. **Learning workflow** — AI summarizes new experiences, user confirms, system deduplicates

## Data Model

```python
class Experience:
    id: str                    # UUID
    content: str               # Natural language description
    category: str              # "guardrail" | "practice" | "lesson"
    severity: str              # "block" | "warn" | "info"
    tags: list[str]            # e.g. ["git", "pr", "teamclaw", "security"]
    scope: dict                # Applicable context (project, tool, operation type)
    source: str                # Origin: which incident/conversation
    embedding: list[float]     # Vector for semantic search
    status: str                # "active" | "deprecated" | "merged"
    created_at: datetime
    updated_at: datetime
```

## API Design

### Core Endpoints

| Endpoint | Method | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `POST /api/check` | Check if an action matches any experiences (intercept) |
| `POST /api/learn` | Submit new experience draft (AI summarizes + dedup check) |
| `POST /api/learn/confirm` | Confirm and persist a draft experience |
| `GET /api/experiences` | List all experiences (with filters) |
| `GET /api/experiences/{id}` | Get single experience |
| `PUT /api/experiences/{id}` | Update experience |
| `DELETE /api/experiences/{id}` | Delete experience |
| `POST /api/experiences/search` | Semantic + keyword hybrid

items.title: Feishu file upload: sanitizeFileNameForUpload causes garbled Chinese filenames
items.content:
## Bug Description

When sending files with Chinese filenames via the Feishu channel, the uploaded file displays with a garbled name (percent-encoded). For example, `测试中文.pdf` appears as `%E6%B5%8B%E8%AF%95%E4%B8%AD%E6%96%87.pdf`.

## Root Cause

In `extensions/feishu/src/media.ts`, the `sanitizeFileNameForUpload` function percent-encodes all non-ASCII characters via `encodeURIComponent()`. However, the Feishu file upload API accepts UTF-8 filenames natively. The encoding is unnecessary and causes garbled display names.

## Expected Behavior

Chinese filenames should be preserved as-is when uploaded to Feishu.

## Actual Behavior

Chinese filenames are percent-encoded, resulting in garbled display names in Feishu.

## Environment

- OpenClaw version: latest
- Channel: Feishu
- OS: WSL2 (Linux)

## Suggested Fix

Pass the original UTF-8 filename directly to the Feishu API without encoding.

items.title: security: Command injection risk in verify.sh base64 prompt handling
items.content:
## Summary

`sh/e2e/lib/verify.sh` constructs remote commands with base64-encoded prompts using an unsafe pattern that could enable command injection if the prompt source changes in the future.

## Location

- `sh/e2e/lib/verify.sh:35` (and similar patterns in other input test functions)

## Vulnerable Pattern

```bash
encoded_prompt=$(printf '%s' "${INPUT_TEST_PROMPT}" | base64 -w 0 2>/dev/null || printf '%s' "${INPUT_TEST_PROMPT}" | base64)
remote_cmd="source ~/.spawnrc 2>/dev/null; \
  export PATH=...; \
  PROMPT=\$(printf '%s' '${encoded_prompt}' | base64 -d); claude -p \"\$PROMPT\""
```

## Issue

The base64-encoded value is embedded directly into a string that undergoes variable expansion. While `INPUT_TEST_PROMPT` is currently hardcoded ("Reply with exactly the text SPAWN_E2E_OK and nothing else."), this pattern is unsafe if:

1. The prompt source changes to accept external input (environment variable, file, API response)
2. A malicious value in `INPUT_TEST_PROMPT` could break out of the single quotes and inject commands

## Example Attack Vector

If `INPUT_TEST_PROMPT` were ever set to:
```
test'; rm -rf /; echo 'pwned
```

The resulting remote command would execute arbitrary code.

## Severity

**CRITICAL** (currently dormant due to hardcoded value, but high-risk pattern)

## Recommended Fix

Pass the prompt via stdin or use a heredoc with proper quoting:

```bash
# Option 1: Base64-encode on the remote side
remote_cmd="source ~/.spawnrc 2>/dev/null; \
  export PATH=...; \
  PROMPT=\$(printf '%s' '${INPUT_TEST_PROMPT}' | base64 -d); claude -p \"\$PROMPT\""

# Option 2: Use a temp file on remote
encoded_prompt=$(printf '%s' "${INPUT_TEST_PROMPT}" | base64)
cloud_exec "${app}" "printf '%s' '${encoded_prompt}' | base64 -d > /tmp/prompt.txt"
remote_cmd="claude -p \"\$(cat /tmp/prompt.txt)\""
```

## Affected Functions

- `input_test_claude()`
- `input_test_codex()`
- `input_test_openclaw()`
- `input_test_zeroclaw()`

---
*Filed automatically by security scan vi

items.title: feat: add runtimeConfig.heartbeat.wakeOnComment to control assignee wakes on comments
items.content:
## Thinking path

Paperclip orchestrates AI agents via adapters (Claude, OpenClaw, etc.) → agents collaborate on issues by posting comments → when a comment is posted, the assignee is unconditionally woken with reason `issue_commented` → in hierarchical orgs (IC → Manager → Director), a reviewer approving work by commenting triggers the submitter to wake, read "approved," find nothing to do, and exit → this burns a full adapter session (model startup, context loading, tool init) for zero value → the fix is a per-agent policy flag that gates this specific wake path while preserving all other wake mechanisms.

## Problem

When any agent posts a comment on an issue, Paperclip unconditionally wakes the assignee with reason `issue_commented`. In multi-layer orgs (IC → Manager → C-Suite → CEO), this creates wasteful wakes: when a reviewer approves work by commenting, the submitting agent wakes up, reads "approved," has nothing to do, and exits — burning a full adapter session for zero value.

With 20 agents in a 4-layer hierarchy, every review cycle generates at least one wasted wake. On expensive models, this adds up fast.

## Solution

Adds `runtimeConfig.heartbeat.wakeOnComment` (boolean, default: `true`) that gates the automatic assignee-comment wake. When `false`, the assignee is **not** woken when someone comments on their issue.

All other wake paths are unaffected:
- `@-mention` wakes (`issue_comment_mentioned`) still fire
- Assignment wakes (`issue_assigned`) still fire
- Reopen-via-comment wakes (`issue_reopened_via_comment`) still fire

This is fully backward-compatible — existing agents behave identically unless `wakeOnComment` is explicitly set to `false`.

## Risks

- **Agent misses a revision request posted as a plain comment (no @-mention, no status change):** Mitigated by the fact that `@-mention` wakes are unaffected. Teams using `wakeOnComment: false` should adopt the convention of `@-mentioning` agents when action is needed, which is already the natura

items.title: RFC: Add Configurable Message Coalescing / Batching for Chat Channels
items.content:
# RFC: Add Configurable Message Coalescing / Batching for Chat Channels

## Summary

OpenClaw should support an **optional, configurable message coalescing window** for inbound chat channels and similar message-based surfaces.

Today, each inbound message is treated as an independent event and can trigger its own agent turn. That behavior is fast, but in real conversational use it often produces the wrong UX: people frequently send thoughts as a burst of short messages, then add an attachment, then clarify what they actually want. OpenClaw can respond too early, before the thought is complete.

This RFC proposes an **opt-in batching layer** between channel ingestion and agent dispatch:

- when a message arrives, OpenClaw waits for a short configurable period
- additional messages from the same sender/session key within that period are appended to the same pending batch
- once the window closes, OpenClaw dispatches **one combined inbound event** into the existing session/agent pipeline
- priority commands and emergency controls can bypass the delay and execute immediately

The goal is not to make OpenClaw slower. The goal is to make it better at **reading the room**.

---

## Motivation

### The problem

On real messaging platforms, humans rarely compose their thoughts as one perfect message.

Common pattern:

1. send a first thought
2. send a follow-up clarification a few seconds later
3. attach a file/image/link
4. finally ask the actual question

OpenClaw currently tends to react to step 1 immediately.

That creates several issues:

1. **Missing context**  
   The agent responds to message 1 before seeing messages 2-5, so it answers an incomplete thought.

2. **Broken conversational flow**  
   The human is still speaking, but the assistant interrupts mid-thought.

3. **Wasted tokens / compute**  
   Instead of one coherent turn, the system spends tokens on multiple partial responses.

4. **Robotic feel**  
   Humans naturally wait a beat to see whether the other

Adds optional per-case `max_latency_ms` and `max_cost_usd` guardrails so a single outlier can fail the gate even when global averages still look healthy.

Current gate checks only global average latency/cost. That can hide catastrophic case-level outliers (exactly the kind of failure teams hit in production with agent workflows).
